steps.title: Track the contrast
steps.content: The text introduces the energy ladder as a common explanation for household fuel choice, then says Nielsen's study shows that model to be reductive. The word "however" signals the author's purpose.

steps.title: Reject misread purposes
steps.content: Choice B overstates the role of country comparison. Choice C reverses the topic by focusing on income effects. Choice D wrongly says the model concerns fuel prices, not household fuel choice.

steps.title: Name the purpose
steps.content: The text's main purpose is to challenge a common explanation for how households choose fuels by showing that other factors also matter. The answer is A.
